title: Extract shared ConcurrencyStep base interface from 7 inconsistent step/event types
acceptanceCriteria: ConcurrencyStep base interface with {tick, description} defined in a shared types file
All 11 step/event types extend ConcurrencyStep
Generic playback functions can accept ConcurrencyStep[] for step counting, description display, etc.
Zero breaking changes to existing engine code (extends is additive)

title: Unify inconsistent naming: Event vs Step vs Snapshot across concurrency types
description:
NAMING INCONSISTENCY: The module uses 4 different naming conventions for the same concept (a single simulation tick's state):

- 'Event' suffix: ThreadEvent, BufferEvent, PhilosopherEvent, RWEvent, BarberEvent (5 types)
- 'Step' suffix: EventLoopStep, GoroutineStep, AsyncStep, MutexStep, DeadlockStep (5 types)
- 'Snapshot' suffix: PoolSnapshot, MutexThreadSnapshot (2 types)
- 'Entry' suffix: ThreadTimelineEntry (1 type)

All describe the same concept: the state at one tick. Convention should be unified. Recommendation: use 'Step' for all top-level per-tick types (matches the playback metaphor of 'stepping through') and 'Snapshot' for nested state captures.

```typescript
// BEFORE (inconsistent)
ThreadEvent, BufferEvent, PhilosopherEvent → ThreadStep, BufferStep, PhilosopherStep
PoolSnapshot → ThreadPoolStep
MutexThreadSnapshot → MutexThreadState (it's a per-thread state, not a full step)
```
acceptanceCriteria: All top-level per-tick types use 'Step' suffix consistently
Nested per-actor state types use 'State' or 'Snapshot' suffix
Deprecated type aliases exported for backwards compatibility during migration
All consuming code updated to new names

title: Extract action union types from inline string literals across engines
description:
TYPE NARROWNESS: 6 engines use inline string literal unions for actions, but these are defined ONLY in the interface field — not as reusable named types:

- ThreadEvent.action: 'read' | 'write' | 'lock' | 'unlock' | 'wait' | 'signal'
- BufferEvent.action: 'produce' | 'consume' | 'wait-full' | 'wait-empty' | 'signal'
- RWEvent.action: 'read-start' | 'read-end' | 'write-start' | 'write-end' | 'wait'
- BarberEvent.customerAction: 'enter' | 'leave-full' | 'wake-barber' | 'sit-wait' | 'get-haircut' | 'done'
- GoroutineStep.action: 'spawn' | 'send' | 'receive' | 'select' | 'done' | 'block'
- PhilosopherEvent.state: 'thinking' | 'hungry' | 'eating' | 'waiting'

Extract as named types:
```typescript
export type ThreadAction = 'read' | 'write' | 'lock' | 'unlock' | 'wait' | 'signal';
export type BufferAction = 'produce' | 'consume' | 'wait-full' | 'wait-empty' | 'signal';
export type PhilosopherState = 'thinking' | 'hungry' | 'eating' | 'waiting';
// etc.
```

This enables: color maps keyed by action type, exhaustive switch statements, reuse in visualization components.
acceptanceCriteria: Named type aliases exported for all 6 action/state union types
Interface fields reference the named types instead of inline literals
Color maps (PHILOSOPHER_STATES, RW_ACTION_COLORS, etc.) are typed with the named action types
Exhaustive switch/if-else is enabled by narrower types

title: Create discriminated union ConcurrencyDemoResult for type-safe demo switching
acceptanceCriteria: ActiveDemoData discriminated union type defined with all 11 demo variants
Only the active demo's simulation data is computed (lazy evaluation)
TypeScript compiler catches mismatched data→viz connections
Switch statement on active demo is exhaustive (TS error if case missed)

title: Type the color constant maps with Record<ActionType, string> instead of Record<string, string>
description:
TYPE NARROWNESS: 8 color maps in ConcurrencyModule.tsx use Record<string, string> as their effective type, losing type safety:

- PHILOSOPHER_STATES: Record<string, string> (should be Record<PhilosopherState, string>)
- HIGHLIGHT_COLORS: Record<string, string> (should be Record<EventLoopStep['highlight'], string>)
- GOROUTINE_STATE_COLORS: Record<Goroutine['state'], string> — GOOD, already typed ✅
- RW_ACTION_COLORS: Record<string, string> (should be Record<RWAction, string>)
- BARBER_ACTION_COLORS: Record<string, string> (should be Record<BarberAction, string>)
- PROMISE_STATE_COLORS: Record<string, string> (should be Record<AsyncPromise['state'], string>)
- DEADLOCK_THREAD_STATE_COLORS: Record<string, string> (should be Record<DeadlockThread['state'], string>)
- MUTEX_STATE_COLORS: Record<string, string> (should be Record<MutexThreadState, string>)

With typed Records, adding a new state without adding its color becomes a TS compile error.
acceptanceCriteria: All 8 color maps typed with the specific union type as key
Adding a new state to any union type produces a TS error if color map is not updated
GOROUTINE_STATE_COLORS pattern replicated across all maps

title: Replace PhilosopherEvent.allForkStates string[] with typed ForkState[]
acceptanceCriteria: allForkStates typed with ForkOwner union instead of string[]
forks typed as tuple [boolean, boolean] instead of boolean[]
forkStateLabels helper function returns ForkOwner[] instead of string[]
Color lookup in visualization uses ForkOwner type

title: Replace JSON.parse(JSON.stringify) deep clone with structuredClone in goroutines.ts
acceptanceCriteria: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify) replaced with structuredClone
Clone function retains generic <T> signature
All 5 goroutine demo functions still produce correct step sequences
No type assertion needed (structuredClone preserves types)

title: Add exhaustive switch for ConcurrencyDemo type in useConcurrencyModule hook
acceptanceCriteria: Both canvas switch and maxSteps switch have exhaustive never checks
Adding a new ConcurrencyDemo variant produces TypeScript compile errors in all relevant switches
Bottom panel switch also has exhaustive check

title: Create shared DemoDef generic type for consistent demo definition across sub-demo systems
acceptanceCriteria: Generic DemoDef<Id> type defined in shared types file
EventLoopDemoDef, GoroutineDemoDef, AsyncPatternDef all extend or use DemoDef
Top-level DemoDef in ConcurrencyModule uses the shared type
New sub-demo systems can reuse the pattern without defining a new type

title: Document data flow pipeline with inline JSDoc showing types at each transformation step
description:
DATA FLOW CLARITY: The complete data pipeline is:
```
User clicks Play → handlePlayPause (ConcurrencyModule.tsx:2472)
  → setInterval at 400ms → setStepIndex(s + 1)
    → active demo data (useMemo) + stepIndex → renders viz component
      → Viz reads events/steps[stepIndex] → renders SVG/DOM
```

This flow is implicit — a new developer must trace through 3000+ lines to understand it. Add a JSDoc comment block at the top of useConcurrencyModule documenting:
1. The complete data flow with types at each step
2. Which state variables drive which UI sections
3. How sub-demo changes (event loop tabs, goroutine tabs) interact with the main step counter
4. The relationship between the 8 useState calls and the 15 useMemo computations
acceptanceCriteria: JSDoc block at top of useConcurrencyModule documents the complete data flow
Each state variable annotated with what UI it drives
Sub-demo interaction pattern documented
A new developer can understand the module architecture from the JSDoc alone
